rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DELORES HEARRING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The decedent's codicil required the creation of a testamentary trust for the benefit of Winston Hearring. The decedent designated the executor and Delores Anderson as co-trustees of the testamentary trust to hold and manage Winston Hearring's 2/5 share of the estate funds to purchase his home. The executor sold the estate's most valuable asset, a 1/5 interest in real property in San Francisco, for $160,000 and distributed the proceeds in equal shares to the 4 devisees (including the executor) without prior court approval of preliminary distribution. Winston Hearring agreed to receive 1/4 of the proceeds, rather than 2/5 given by the codicil, in exchange for immediate outright distribution to purchase his home in Texas. The estate funds on hand in the total amount of $5,486 will be exhausted for partial payment of statutory fees of the executor's former and/or current attorneys. There will be no estate funds left to create and fund the testamentary trust for Winston Hearring. Probate Code section 15202 provides that "a trust is created only if there is trust property". 2) The executor's former attorney has not waived partial statutory fees for services rendered to the executor before the former attorney was discharged in 2021. Section 10814 and CRC 7.704 require the statutory fees to be apportioned between both attorneys according to the services actually rendered by each attorney or as agreed to by the attorneys. No agreement has been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF PATRICIA MADAUHS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Substituted Judgment Seeking Court Authorization For The Conservator To Pay For The Travel Expenses Of The Conservatee'S Sister To Visit
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared/addressed at the hearing: (1) Need further allegation of how this pre-granted reimbursement falls under a gift/donation of Prob. Code sec.2580(b)(1). (2) Need a proposed budget with proof for the travel expenses. The broad discretion appears to be premature without the past accounting from the removed conservator and the current accounting from the successor conservator. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance requried, despite the following issues: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No proof of service of a copy of a proposed order is on file. CRC 3.1362(d). 3) The proof of service attached to the notice of motion shows a copy was served on 02/28/2024, before the clerk accepted and processed the filing on 03/04/2024 and assigned this hearing date. The client and all other parties who have appeared must receive a copy of the notice including the hearing date. 4) Please provide the client's telephone number that should be listed at item 6 of the proposed order. 5) Has James Feilders, an unrepresented party who has appeared, consented to electronic service? rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTOPHER MORRIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/15/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The real property was sold for a loss of $5,000, but more than $97,000 was paid to various parties, primarily to Keith Race, to make repairs to the property. Keith Race's invoice dated 11/9/23 shows that he was paid $30,000 "Deposits" on his $59,025 total bill. If the estate paid him a $30,000 deposit on his services, the total repairs to the property would have exceeded $127,000. Why, after so much effort, did the house sell for a loss? Was the probate referee's appraisal overvalued? (2) Attorney costs: (a) the initial filing fee was $435, not $450; (b) the filing fee for the ex parte petition to appoint a guardian ad litem was $60, not $66.73; (c) local parking costs are not reimbursable [LRSF 14.57 B.]; (d) there were no proofs of service filed on 9/28/23 or 10/1/23. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/6/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WYN WANG LEUNG IRREVOCABLE TRUST DATED APRIL 20, TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The first accounting petition includes not only the trust accounting, but also an accounting of non-trust assets such as the settlor's IRA and other retirement accounts and report of the petitioner's acts taken as attorney-in-fact and agent under the settlor's power of attorney. The petitioner has not opened a new case (PES) under the name of the power of attorney, nor has the petitioner filed a separate POA petition under Probate Code section 4541(b), to seek approval of her accounting with respect to the non-trust assets covered by the power of attorney, rather than the trust, despite the Court's directive so given at the previous two hearings on 01/16/2024 & 03/26/2024. The Court is to determine whether to take all accounting petitions off calendar at this time for lack of diligence. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To Invalidate Teh Williams Family Trust Of Betty Yvonne Williams And Leroy Williams And To Remove The Appointed Trustee, Shawny K.
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural issues with the petition remain: 1) At least 30 days before the time set for hearing on the petition, the petitioner shall cause notice of the hearing to be delivered pursuant to Section 1215 to all trustees and all beneficiaries (Probate Code Section 17203). Form DE-120 is required for this proceeding. 2) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 3) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries (CRC 7.902). 4)No proposed order has been received (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WENTZ FAMILY LIVING TRUST DATED MARCH 14, 2011
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For 1. Elder Financial Abuse Under Cal. Welf. & Inst Code Section 15600 Et Seq 2. Removal Of Trustee Pursuant To Probate Code 15642 Et Seq. And 17200 Et Seq. 3. Accounting Pursuant To Probate Code Sections 10950 Et Seq. 16320 Et Seq. And 17200 Et Seq. 4. Surcharge Pursuant To Probate Code Sections 16420 Et Seq. And 17200 Et Seq. 5. Recovery Of Property Taken Pursuant To Probate Code 850 Et Seq. 6. Breach Of Fiduciary Duty 7. Negligence /Negligence Per Se 8. Constructive Trust 9
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/21/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must be given directly to a party unless the party is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person. If an interested party is represented by an attorney, notice must ALSO be sent to the attorney, but not only to the attorney, unless so directed. [CRC 7.51(a) and (b).] (2) A copy of the power of attorney appointing Scott Seebach Wentz as Lydia Seebach Wentz's attorney-in-fact must be filed. Without such substantiation, it appears that Brooke Marie Wentz is the designated attorney-in-fact pursuant to the durable power of attorney executed on 3/14/11. (3) If Lydia Seebach Wentz is not actually a petitioner (she did not sign/verify this petition), she must also receive notice as the current vested beneficiary. (4) The petition does not address whether or not the other trustor, Keith Edward Wentz, is deceased and, if so, when he died. (5) The attorney's declaration filed on 2/22/24 was not e-filed until 2/23/24. Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] A supplement to a petition filed pursuant to Probate Code section 850 must be served in the same manner as the 850 petition, meaning that a copy of the supplement must be served. (6) The proof of service of the Notice of Hearing (DE-120) is incomplete at item 3. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/10/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Probate Code Section 10950 applies to personal representatives. The examiner is aware that a response was filed on 4/9/24. No courtesy copy has been received yet, as of 4/12/24. Please do not put stickers on the face of the courtesy copies provided to the Court. Only one set of copies is necessary, which copies are reviewed by an examiner and then by the judge.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MILTON FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Orders Appointing Successor Trustee And Setting Bond
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/14/24. Prior to a full review of the petition, a verified declaration must be filed addressing venue: Petitioner cites 17005(c) for venue, but the proper code section is 17005(b). Please allege whether there are any trust assets located in San Francisco, and, if so, please specify the asset and specific location. All documents to be filed by 4/29/24.
